channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: California Professor Acquitted of Assault After Removing Tear Gas at Immigration Protest
item.description: Jonathan Caravello, a California philosophy professor, was acquitted of charges for assaulting federal agents after removing a tear gas canister during a protest against an immigration raid. Caravello...

item.title: House Republicans Block Democratic Effort to End US Military Actions Against Iran
item.description: US House Republicans blocked a Democratic effort on Thursday, April 9, to end American military attacks on Iran, continuing their party's pattern of preventing constraints on President Donald Trump's ...

item.title: Four International Stars Lead 2025-26 NBA MVP Race
item.description: Four international superstars—Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Victor Wembanyama, Nikola Jokic, and Luka Doncic—are the top contenders for the 2025-26 NBA Most Valuable Player award as the regular season near...

item.title: Trump attacks conservative podcasters Tucker Carlson, Megyn Kelly over Iran war criticism
item.description: US President Donald Trump forcefully criticised several prominent conservative commentators who have voiced opposition to his military action against Iran, branding them as attention-seeking 'NUT JOBS...

item.title: WHO strengthens global health network to prepare for next pandemic
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) don raise alarm say another pandemic fit happen very soon. Dem highlight say the world dey face plenty challenges like climate change, wahala (conflict), and health...

item.title: Mercedes-Benz A200 Night Edition Lands in South Africa with Exclusive Styling
item.description: Mercedes-Benz has officially launched the A200 Night Edition in South Africa, joining its CLA sibling which arrived earlier this year. This stylish four-door hatchback boasts a distinctive look thanks...

item.title: Melania Trump clarifies she was not introduced to Donald Trump by Jeffrey Epstein, matchmaker offers testimony
item.description: Melania Trump has publicly denied rumours linking her introduction to Donald Trump with the convicted pedophile Jeffrey Epstein. During a statement at the White House, she asserted she was neither a f...

item.title: Gabi Motuba: Jazz, Spirituality and Resistance in Contemporary South Africa
item.description: Gabi Motuba, a Pretoria-born jazz vocalist, composer and educator, has been recognised with the 2026 Standard Bank Young Artist Award for Jazz, reflecting her innovative contribution to South African ...

item.title: Five individuals from Jammu and Kashmir perish in a road accident in Kuwait
item.description: A tragic road accident in Kuwait claimed the lives of five people hailing from Jammu and Kashmir. The incident occurred when their vehicle was involved in a fatal collision, leaving no survivors. Auth...

item.title: India-bound oil tankers resume limited transit through Hormuz Strait during ceasefire
item.description: On the second day of the ceasefire in Iran, maritime movement through the Strait of Hormuz has nearly come to a standstill after briefly rising to about ten percent of pre-war levels. Ship tracking da...

item.title: Philippines supports international appeal to safeguard UN peacekeepers amid rising violence in Lebanon
item.description: The Philippines has expressed strong support for United Nations peacekeepers deployed in Lebanon, joining more than 65 nations in urging enhanced protection for these personnel following fatal attacks...

item.title: Delta State hikes doctors’ salaries and allowances to retain medical professionals
item.description: Delta State government don take serious step to solve the wahala of doctors waka waka abroad, the so-called 'Japa' trend. Governor Sheriff Oborevwori approve say entry-level doctors go dey collect ₦57...

item.title: Documentary 'Agatha's Almanac' Highlights the Simple Life of 92-Year-Old Farmer in Manitoba
item.description: The award-winning documentary *Agatha’s Almanac* offers an intimate look into the life of 92-year-old Agatha Bock, who continues to maintain her ancestral family farm in southern Manitoba. Directed by...

item.title: Voter Shift to Minor Parties Amid Lack of Major Party Policy Detail
item.description: Pauline Hanson's One Nation (PHON) has seen a significant rise in support, which is reflected not only in opinion polls but also in actual votes, notably in South Australia. The party is typically reg...

item.title: Madhya Pradesh's Uniform Civil Code Proposal Faces Tribal Resistance
item.description: The state of Madhya Pradesh in India has proposed a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) aimed at standardizing laws governing personal matters such as marriage, divorce, and inheritance across all communities. H...

item.title: Hakimi Reflects on Morocco’s Behaviour and AFCON Final Fallout
item.description: Achraf Hakimi, the captain of Morocco’s national football team, don open up say e dey disappointed with how him countrymen behave during the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) final against Senegal. T...

item.title: Overview of Kenya's Ministry of Education Leadership and Departments in 2026
item.description: Kenya's Ministry of Education oversees formulation, implementation, and monitoring of policies, standards, and curricula for all education levels. As of 2026, the Ministry is headed by Cabinet Secreta...

item.title: EFF adopts ward-based strategy for 2026 Johannesburg local elections, prioritising manifesto over mayoral candidate
item.description: The Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) has announced that it will not name a mayoral candidate for Johannesburg ahead of the 2026 local government elections. Instead, the party will focus its campaign on...

item.title: The benefits and risks of children's video gaming: What parents should know
item.description: As research increasingly highlights the developmental benefits of video games for children, experts are urging parents not to hastily ban gaming. Studies suggest that, when managed properly, gaming ca...

item.title: 9ice questions Nigeria’s heavy religious culture, says prayers alone no fit bring national progress
item.description: Veteran Nigerian musician 9ice, wey him real name be Abolore Adegbola Akande, don spark serious gist online after he talk say years of praying to Jesus and Allah never make Nigeria better. For a viral...
